Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Q16 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements in reference to transformation of stresses:
1. In case of two like and equal principal stresses, the resultant stress on any plane is equal to the principal stresses.
2. In case of two equal and unlike stresses, the resultant stress on any plane is equal to principal stresses.
3. In case of two like and equal principal stresses, the obliquity of the resultant stress is on any plane is zero.
4. In a strained material subjected to two dimensional stresses, the sum of normal stresses on any two mutually perpendicular planes is equal to the sum of major and minor principal stresses.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q19 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. When a shaft is subjected to torsion, the actual shear stress is developed on the cross - section in the vertical direction.
2. A horizontal shaft when subjected to torsion, complimentary shear stress gets produced on the longitudinal section having the axis, longitudinally.
3. When a shaft is subjected to torsion, maximum shear stress occurs at half of the radius measured from the axis of the shaft.
4. For two equally strong shafts, their polar moduli are also equal.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Q34 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Shear stresses on two mutually normal planes are equal in magnitude and direction.
2. The sum of normal stresses on mutually normal planes is equal to the sum of principal stresses.
Of the above statements, which is / are true ?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Shear stresses on two mutually normal i.e. perpendicular planes are equal in magnitude but opposite in direction.

exceeds a critical value, yielding of material initiates.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Not attempted

Statement (II): Algebraic sum of all the forces either on left or right of the section is shear force.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Q37 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): In an elastic body, the amount of elastic deformation under a given stress is more in case of tensile stress as compared to compressive stress.
Statement (II): All the elastic constants are assumed as same in both tension and compression.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Statement I is false as elastic deformation under a given stress in an elastic body is considered as same both in tension and compression. Statement II is true.

Q38 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Statement (I): Strain is fundamental concept and stress is the derived concept.
Statement (II): Strain is dimensionless while stress has dimensions of pressure.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Both Statement I and Statement II are true but Statement II doesn't explain the Statement I.
